baseline.name: Baseline
baseline.description: A grayscale baseline — pure white, black, and 10 shades in between. Numeric and word aliases (e.g. `5` and `five`) make these legible in any rule.

elementary.name: elementary
elementary.description: The color palette behind elementary OS — fruit-named primaries with 5 tonal steps each, plus paired Text values for accessible body copy.

flatui.name: Flat UI
flatui.description: The flat-design colors popularised by Flat UI Colors — 20 named hues with no shade variants.

goddardhale.name: Goddard & Hale
goddardhale.description: Lewis Goddard's personal palette — five accent hues plus warm neutrals, expanded into 20 perceptually-graded shades.

flexoki.name: Flexoki
flexoki.description: Steph Ango's inky color scheme for prose and code, derived in Oklab for perceptual evenness across light and dark modes.

material.name: Material
material.description: Google's Material Design palette — 19 hues, each with a 50→900 ramp plus four A-prefixed accent tones.

ubuntu.name: Ubuntu
ubuntu.description: Canonical's Ubuntu brand colors — the famous Orange and four Aubergines with 13 tonal steps each.
